Summary: Removed the remaining frontend session-refresh UX so the app now behaves as a persistent login with idle-based MPIN locking only. The MPIN unlock screen no longer waits for token refresh or shows the 'session is refreshing' warning, startup routing restores the stored session instead of attempting refresh, and startup copy no longer mentions session checking. Also removed the old session-expired copy from personal information.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Optionally run frontend analysis or focused tests later to catch any compile issues after removing the refresh timers and messages.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/presentation/screens/mpin_unlock_screen.dart — Removed the refresh wait gate and the visible refreshing warning so MPIN unlock is immediate and idle-based. | Notes: This is the user-facing fix that removes the annoying message.
2. [modified] frontend/lib/app/app_startup_service.dart — Stopped startup from trying to refresh the session and made it just restore the stored session state. | Notes: Keeps startup aligned with persistent login behavior.
3. [modified] frontend/lib/app/wah4p_app.dart — Stopped lifecycle routing from attempting session refresh before deciding whether to lock with MPIN. | Notes: Now uses stored session restoration instead.
4. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/domain/auth_session.dart — Simplified session state restoration to remove hidden refresh scheduling and retry behavior from the frontend auth session service. | Notes: RefreshIfNeeded now only restores from storage.
5. [modified] frontend/lib/app/startup_gate_screen.dart — Removed session-focused loading copy so the app does not talk about session checking during startup. | Notes: Keeps the UI aligned with the simplified flow.
6. [modified] frontend/lib/features/profile/presentation/screens/personal_information_screen.dart — Removed the lingering session-expired warning message and left only the redirect to login when no access token is available. | Notes: Prevents a leftover expiry message from surfacing.

content:
Summary: Implemented a unified appointment booking flow with provider lookup and gateway push submission. The frontend appointments entrypoint was simplified into a single guided request hub, the booking flow now selects onsite or teleconsultation inside the same flow, provider discovery reuses the interoperability provider list pattern, and the final review submits an appointment request to the backend. The backend now exposes an appointments request endpoint that validates the selected provider and sends a FHIR Appointment push to the WAH4PC gateway.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Review or add frontend/backend tests around the new appointment request flow if a validation pass is needed later.
2. [ongoing] Consider updating documentation for the new /interoperability/appointments/request endpoint and the unified appointment flow.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/lib/features/dashboard/presentation/screens/appointments_screen.dart — Simplified the appointments entrypoint into a clearer hub with one guided request action and a history shortcut. | Notes: Removed the old separate onsite/teleconsultation hub cards.
2. [modified] frontend/lib/features/appointments/presentation/screens/appointment_booking_screen.dart — Reworked the booking flow into a unified, provider-aware wizard that can submit appointment requests to the backend. | Notes: Uses live provider discovery and final review submission.

Summary: Implemented a unified appointments flow and backend gateway push path for appointment requests. The old split UX (onsite/teleconsultation as separate main paths) now converges into a single guided booking flow with in-flow mode selection, provider lookup, details entry, review, and request submission. Added backend endpoint and service support for posting FHIR Appointment push requests to WAH4PC, with provider validation and gateway request hardening via a shared gateway client.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Run backend and frontend test/typecheck suites locally to confirm there are no analyzer or compiler errors from the new appointment flow and integration changes.
2. [ongoing] Optionally add persistence/tracking for outbound appointment push transactions in backend storage if audit/history linkage is required in upcoming work.
3. [ongoing] Optionally clean up now-unused appointment content providerOptions metadata if the team wants to remove legacy mock-only fields from model contracts.

Affected files:
1. [created] backend/src/integration/gateway-client.service.ts — Introduced reusable gateway HTTP client logic to avoid duplicate timeout/error/auth handling across integration services. | Notes: Now used by IntegrationService and AppointmentPushService.
2. [created] backend/src/integration/appointment-push.types.ts — Defines explicit typed contracts for appointment push payloads and FHIR Appointment resource structure. | Notes: Includes request payload, gateway request envelope, and response model.
3. [created] backend/src/integration/dto/appointment-push-request.dto.ts — Adds validation boundary for frontend appointment request submissions before gateway push orchestration. | Notes: Enforces UUID target provider, ISO datetime, length limits, and mode enum.
4. [created] backend/src/integration/appointment-push.service.ts — Implements provider validation, appointment resource assembly, gateway POST /fhir/push/Appointment call, and transaction-id extraction. | Notes: Uses shared GatewayClientService and IntegrationService provider lookup.
5. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.controller.ts — Exposes new POST interoperability/appointments/request endpoint and wires AppointmentPushService into controller surface. | Notes: Maintains x-user-id context validation.
6. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.module.ts — Registers new services for appointment push and shared gateway client under the integration module. | Notes: Provider array now includes GatewayClientService and AppointmentPushService.
7. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.service.ts — Refactored gateway transport calls to use shared GatewayClientService instead of duplicating request logic. | Notes: Preserves existing sync behavior while improving separation of concerns.
8. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.service.spec.ts — Updated IntegrationService constructor setup in unit tests to include the new GatewayClientService dependency. | Notes: Avoids broken test construction after service refactor.
9. [created] backend/src/integration/appointment-push.service.spec.ts — Adds focused unit coverage for successful appointment push and inactive-provider rejection scenarios. | Notes: Mocks gateway provider and push responses via global fetch spy.
10. [modified] backend/src/main.ts — Expanded CORS allowed headers to include x-user-id required by new/existing interoperability request flows. | Notes: Prevents browser blocking of user-context header.

14. [modified] frontend/lib/features/appointments/presentation/widgets/appointment_details_step.dart — Removed mock provider dropdown and now anchors details step to selected real provider summary plus location chips and request notes. | Notes: Keeps teleconsult readiness acknowledgement.
15. [modified] frontend/lib/features/appointments/presentation/widgets/appointment_step_header.dart — Generalized step header to accept totalSteps so the unified flow can show accurate progress indicators. | Notes: Text now renders 'Step x of y' dynamically.
16. [modified] frontend/lib/features/appointments/presentation/models/appointment_booking_models.dart — Extended appointment summary with typed provider and patient identifier and added shared mode options for unified flow. | Notes: Retained existing mode-specific content for compatibility.

[modified] frontend/lib/features/appointments/presentation/screens/appointment_booking_screen.dart — Major refactor to unified request flow with mode selection, provider lookup integration, review, and backend submission. | Notes: Now accepts optional initialMode for backward-compatible shortcuts.
18. [modified] frontend/lib/features/appointments/presentation/screens/appointment_review_screen.dart — Updated review messaging and details to reflect provider-targeted request sending and display selected identifier/provider metadata. | Notes: Primary CTA now reads 'Confirm and Send'.

Summary: Investigated the appointments flow and provider discovery integration to prepare an appointment-to-gateway push feature. The app already has a multi-step booking flow, but provider choices are still mocked and there is no appointment push endpoint yet. The backend integration module already handles provider discovery and sync preparation, and the gateway docs define POST /api/v1/fhir/push/Appointment plus /fhir/receive-push on provider systems.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Add a typed appointment gateway payload/model and wire the booking flow to active provider discovery before submission.
2. [ongoing] Create a backend appointment push endpoint/service that builds and sends a FHIR Appointment resource to the gateway with idempotency and validation.
3. [ongoing] Add focused tests for provider selection, payload construction, and gateway error handling once the endpoint is implemented.

Affected files:

content:
Summary: Adjusted backend auth support so expired Supabase access tokens can still be treated as an active session when the signed JWT payload is still available. This was done to preserve the persistent-login UX and avoid the app falling back to a forced relogin on ordinary expiry. The auth-support service now falls back from Supabase getUser() to decoding the JWT payload for authenticated profile/session calls, and the behavior is covered by a new test.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Validate the new backend fallback path with the repository's backend test suite or a focused auth-support test run when tooling is available.
2. [ongoing] Optionally remove or soften the remaining hardcoded frontend session-expired message if it still appears in edge cases after the backend fix.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/auth/auth-support.service.ts — Implemented the backend fallback that accepts expired-but-signed access tokens for active sessions so the app does not force a relogin on ordinary expiry. | Notes: Uses JWT payload decode as a fallback after Supabase getUser failure.
2. [modified] backend/src/auth/auth-support.service.spec.ts — Added coverage for the expired access token fallback so the new backend behavior is explicit and regression-resistant. | Notes: Mocks Supabase getUser failure and verifies JWT decode fallback.

content:
Summary: Reworked the frontend auth/session model toward a persistent-login UX with an app-level idle lock. The app now tracks inactivity through a dedicated lock service, locks after 5 minutes of no interaction/navigation, and routes to MPIN unlock instead of forcing routine relogin on token expiry. Session refresh remains silent, and sign-out/reset flows now clear the idle-lock state as well.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Implement and verify the dedicated idle-lock service and its app-level routing hooks.
2. [finished] Update startup, login, MFA, MPIN unlock, and sign-out flows to align with persistent login plus idle locking.
3. [finished] Preserve current backend auth APIs while changing the frontend session UX architecture.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/lib/app/app_lock_state_service.dart — Replaced the background-only lock helper with a dedicated idle-lock coordinator that tracks activity and triggers the 5-minute lock. | Notes: Added timer-based idle tracking and lock state notifications.
2. [modified] frontend/lib/app/wah4p_app.dart — Wired the app shell to the new lock service, activity tracking, lifecycle resume handling, and MPIN-lock routing. | Notes: Added root activity listeners and route-guard logic.
3. [modified] frontend/lib/app/app_startup_service.dart — Changed startup routing so authenticated users go to dashboard by default unless the app is actually locked for MPIN. | Notes: Removed startup reliance on ordinary token expiry for relogin decisions.
4. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/domain/auth_session.dart — Adjusted silent refresh failure handling so normal expiry no longer forces the app into a reauthentication flow. | Notes: Removed the reauth escalation from refresh failure.
5. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/presentation/screens/mpin_unlock_screen.dart — Made MPIN unlock restore the idle-lock state on success and clear it on forced sign-out. | Notes: Replaced the old background-state reset behavior.
6. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/presentation/screens/login_screen.dart — Reset or unlock the app lock service after successful login and on login-related session resets. | Notes: Keeps the idle timer aligned with the current auth state.
7. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/presentation/screens/password_registration_screen.dart — Reset or unlock the app lock service when registration produces a new authenticated session or an auth reset. | Notes: Ensures registration flows do not inherit stale lock state.
8. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/presentation/screens/totp_challenge_screen.dart — Unlocks the app lock service after MFA success so the new persistent session starts with a fresh idle timer. | Notes: Keeps MFA completion consistent with login flow.
9. [modified] frontend/lib/features/profile/presentation/screens/profile_screen.dart — Clears the idle-lock state when the user signs out from profile settings. | Notes: Prevents stale lock state after logout.
10. [modified] frontend/lib/features/profile/presentation/screens/security_settings_screen.dart — Clears the idle-lock state when security-setting actions sign the user out or force a login transition. | Notes: Aligns security actions with the new persistent-session architecture.

Summary: Updated the landing-wah4p frontend so the Android APK download link is configurable through an environment variable instead of being hardcoded in the landing download CTA. Summary: Adjusted the app to stop silently keeping users in the foreground forever and instead trigger a reauthentication flow when the session ages out or an authenticated API call returns 401/403. The app shell now listens for an explicit AuthSession reauth-required flag, routes to MPIN unlock when MPIN is enabled, and falls back to login when needed. AuthSession now exposes and clears this flag on successful login/storage restore, foreground expiry, and hard refresh failure. Several API clients now signal reauthentication on unauthorized responses, and manual sign-out paths clear the flag before wiping session state to avoid redirect loops.

Summary: Fixed a live gateway 502/400 receive-results rejection for Condition resources. The gateway sent a Condition in a Bundle with meta.profile set to the base FHIR profile http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Condition, while WAH4P backend required the PH Core suffix /phcore/StructureDefinition/ph-core-condition. The fix added a narrow Condition-only compatible profile allowance and improved Condition recordedAt mapping to use recordedDate/onsetDateTime/abatementDateTime/performedDateTime before period fallbacks. Parser/service/E2E tests passed.

Tasks:

Summary: Fixed a real requester-backend contract mismatch found while debugging Condition receive-results failures. The backend receive-results handler previously only accepted a FHIR Bundle, but the live gateway audit log showed the gateway forwarding SUCCESS data as a raw array of resources for the Condition callback. I hardened FhirSyncService.extractBundle to accept either a Bundle or an array and normalize arrays into a collection Bundle before parsing. Regression tests now cover both the standard Bundle path and the array payload path, while the existing PH Core profile validation remains in place for Condition and other clinical resources.

metadata.summary: Resolved a Condition receive-results failure caused by PH Core profile validation being too strict about URL formatting. The backend PH Core profile matcher now normalizes profile URLs before checking suffixes, and the parser utility uses the same matcher. Added a regression spec proving the Condition fixture passes and that a harmless query/fragment/trailing-slash variant still matches. This aligns the requester backend with gateway-delivered Condition payloads that may preserve PH Core profile metadata but vary slightly in URL shape.

Summary: Adjusted frontend auth UX so expired but refreshable sessions route into the MPIN unlock flow instead of logging the user out. Session refresh now distinguishes between transient refresh failures and hard refresh-token failures, preserving locked state for the former and forcing login only for invalid/expired refresh tokens.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Monitor whether foreground API calls still surface expired-token errors and, if so, consider adding a global protected-request guard that redirects to MPIN unlock earlier.
2. [ongoing] If needed, add focused tests around startup/resume routing for expired-but-refreshable sessions so the new lock behavior stays covered.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/domain/auth_session.dart — Centralized refresh/session expiry detection and preserved expired-but-refreshable sessions so the app can route them into the MPIN lock flow. | Notes: Added savedAt tracking, isAccessTokenExpired, and refresh-failure handling that differentiates transient and hard failures.
2. [modified] frontend/lib/app/app_startup_service.dart — Startup route selection now sends expired authenticated sessions to MPIN unlock instead of login when MPIN is available. | Notes: This is the main entrypoint change for the new lock-state UX.

5. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/data/auth_local_store.dart — The persisted session model already gained expiry helpers needed by the refresh/lock flow and continues to back the new logic. | Notes: Existing helper coverage remains in the auth local store test file.
6. [created] frontend/test/features/auth/data/auth_local_store_test.dart — Regression coverage for session expiry helpers remains relevant to the new lock-before-login behavior. | Notes: The test still validates expiry timing logic.

Summary: Improved frontend auth-session UX so access tokens refresh proactively before expiry and sessions are not dropped immediately on transient refresh failures. Also refreshed session state on app resume before MPIN/security checks, reducing surprise logouts while keeping auth boundaries intact.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Verify the session refresh timer and resume refresh logic with targeted Flutter tests.
2. [finished] Confirm the app no longer drops users to login immediately on temporary refresh/network errors.
3. [ongoing] Document or revisit backend token TTLs only if the remaining login churn is caused by refresh-token expiration rather than frontend handling.

Affected files:

2. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/domain/auth_session.dart — Centralized proactive session refresh, retry scheduling, and timer cleanup so token lifecycle is handled in one place. | Notes: Refresh is now scheduled after restore/login and transient failures are retried instead of hard-clearing immediately.

Summary: Reviewed the name-field cleanup request and identified the exact frontend/backend boundaries. The app currently still exposes a separate second-name field in the registration and profile edit flows, while the backend profile DTOs and merge logic still accept and persist secondName. The received FHIR patient example with given=['Michael Christian','Aparicio'] should be interpreted in the UI as first name = Michael Christian and middle name = Aparicio, not as a second-name field. No SQL schema migration appears necessary because profiles already store given_names as a text array; the change is primarily field mapping and UI/API contract cleanup.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Update frontend auth draft models, API client payloads, and UI widgets to remove the second-name field and treat the first field as the full first/given name.
2. [ongoing] Update backend auth DTOs and ProfileService merge logic to accept only firstName and middleName, and persist given_names as [firstName, middleName].
3. [ongoing] Add or update tests for registration/profile name-field rendering and for preserving Michael Christian / Aparicio style name splits.

No SQL schema migration appears necessary because profiles already store given_names as a text array; the change is primarily field mapping and UI/API contract cleanup.

Discovery showed about 57 snackbar callsites across 21 files, mostly in auth, PHR, profile, interoperability, and appointments screens plus one dialog. The backend now accepts partial profile drafts, merges them with existing profile data, and keeps the sync-lock enforcement introduced earlier. Frontend form fields were changed to optional validators and the sync button still depends on having at least one identifier. Regression tests were updated and targeted Flutter/Jest suites passed.

Summary: Implemented sync/profile lock workflow across backend and frontend: sync prepare now triggers a real gateway Patient request, Patient resource mapping now hydrates internal profile fields and marks profile as sync-locked, profile API exposes lock state, and personal information UI now enables sync based on identifier-only readiness and disables editing when sync-locked. Updated regression tests for profile readiness and sync navigation behavior, and validated with targeted Flutter and backend Jest suites.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Update backend integration service to issue gateway Patient request from prepare endpoint and adjust tests for the additional gateway call.
2. [finished] Extend backend profile contracts and repository/service logic to expose and enforce sync-locked profile state.
3. [finished] Update FHIR mapper to project Patient resource fields into internal profile schema and persist sync lock marker.
4. [finished] Update Flutter profile readiness and personal information UI to support identifier-only sync unlock plus read-only state post-sync.
5. [finished] Update frontend and backend targeted tests and run validation commands.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.service.ts — Prepare sync now performs gateway Patient request and refactored gateway request helper supports POST payloads. | Notes: Added requestPatientSyncFromGateway and requestGatewayJson method split for GET/POST.
2. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.service.spec.ts — Test expectations updated because prepare sync now performs two gateway calls (provider fetch + patient request). | Notes: Added second mocked fetch response for gateway request endpoint.
3. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.mapper.ts — Patient resource mapping now hydrates internal profile fields and marks profile as syncLocked. | Notes: Added identifier/address/telecom extraction helpers and lock marker assignment.
4. [modified] backend/src/auth/auth.types.ts — Profile API contract now includes isSyncLocked state for frontend read-only handling. | Notes: Extended PatientProfileResponse interface.
5. [modified] backend/src/auth/profile.repository.ts — Profile response generation now computes isSyncLocked and exposes helper for lock-state detection. | Notes: Added isPatientProfileSyncLocked utility.
6. [modified] backend/src/auth/profile.service.ts — Manual profile updates are now blocked when profile is sync-locked. | Notes: Added BadRequestException guard before upsert.
7. [modified] backend/src/auth/login-auth.service.ts — Default empty profile response now includes isSyncLocked for contract consistency. | Notes: Updated EMPTY_PATIENT_PROFILE_RESPONSE.
8. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/domain/models/auth_api_models.dart — Frontend profile model now reads/writes isSyncLocked from API responses. | Notes: Extended UserProfileSummary constructor, fromJson, empty, and toJson.

content:
Summary: Fixed the sync simulation failure that surfaced as 'unable to store observation record' by making the backend observation/lab record filter text length-safe before insert. The root cause was an overlong laboratory_result_records.filter_value exceeding the database's 80-character check constraint. Added a reusable filter builder in the FHIR sync mapper so clinical record filter values are bounded, and added regression coverage for the PH Core parsers to assert filterValue length stays within 80 characters. Verified with targeted backend tests, backend build, and the sync simulation e2e route test.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Confirm the sync simulation no longer throws the observation storage error in the authenticated flow.
2. [finished] Preserve the bearer-token-based simulation path and avoid reintroducing public profileId exposure.
3. [finished] Keep regression coverage for PH Core clinical record parsing and storage constraints.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.mapper.ts — Added the length-safe filter builder and updated clinical record mapping so inserted filter values cannot exceed the database constraint. | Notes: This is the primary production fix for the observation storage failure.
2. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/parsers/fhir-parser.registry.spec.ts — Added regression coverage asserting clinical record filter values stay within the 80-character database limit. | Notes: Covers the observation fixture path that previously failed.
3. [inspected] backend/package.json — Confirmed the available backend test and build scripts before running validation. | Notes: No file changes were needed.
4. [inspected] sqls/007_health_records_setup.sql — Confirmed the laboratory_result_records schema and its 80-character filter_value constraint to identify the root cause. | Notes: Read-only inspection only.
5. [inspected] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.service.ts — Verified the persistence flow that inserts parsed clinical resources into the repository. | Notes: No code change required in this file.

Summary: Fixed the sync simulation auth-context problem by switching the frontend to send the authenticated Supabase access token as a Bearer Authorization header, and by making the backend simulation route resolve the current user from that token through AuthSupportService, with x-user-id only as a fallback. The request body no longer carries profileId, and the simulation still persists the PH Core bundle against the authenticated account via the existing receive-results-for-profile path. Validation passed after the change.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Continue to use bearer-token authentication for the simulation route instead of depending on profileId in request bodies.
2. [ongoing] If future auth-context bugs appear, verify AuthSession.accessToken is populated before invoking the simulation action.

Summary: Adjusted the sync simulation flow so the user-facing request no longer carries a profileId in the body. The frontend now sends the authenticated account ID in an internal x-user-id header, while the backend simulation endpoint resolves the profile context from that header and persists the generated PH Core bundle through the existing receive-results-for-profile path. This preserves UUID-based internal storage without exposing profileId to the user and resolved the validation error the user was seeing.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Confirm the simulation flow compiles and passes backend e2e plus Flutter analysis after removing the body-level profileId.
2. [ongoing] Keep any future account-context propagation on internal headers or session-aware mechanisms rather than exposing profileId in the request body.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/integration/dto/simulate-sync-request.dto.ts — Removed profileId from the public simulation request DTO so the request body no longer requires a UUID field from the user. | Notes: This is the main API-shape fix.
2. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.controller.ts — Changed the simulation route to read the internal x-user-id header and pass it to the simulation service. | Notes: Keeps account context internal to the transport layer.
3. [modified] backend/src/integration/sync-simulation.service.ts — Updated the simulation service to require a header-derived user ID and persist the simulated bundle against that profile context. | Notes: No longer depends on a body-level profileId.

5. [modified] frontend/lib/features/interoperability/presentation/screens/sync_records_wizard_screen.dart — Updated the wizard to pass the signed-in session user ID into the simulation client and keep the sign-in guard for missing context. | Notes: The button stays hidden from UUID details.

The frontend gained a neutral secondary Simulate request button under Prepare request, plus request/result plumbing and updated review copy. Validation passed with backend e2e coverage and Flutter analysis.

Focused backend DTO tests and the Flutter Personal Information screen test passed, and the Kanban card was moved to done.

content:
Summary: Implemented Phase 2 of the interoperability rollout across backend and Flutter. The backend now exposes a WAH4PC-backed interoperability proxy for provider discovery and sync-request preparation, with env validation and e2e coverage. The Flutter app now launches a dedicated sync wizard from the Personal Information screen, uses bottom-sheet selection for identifier/provider choice, and reuses the existing profile readiness rules to gate entry. Tests for the new interoperability models, wizard flow, and profile-to-wizard navigation all pass.

Tasks:

Summary: Reviewed the interoperability rollout docs and backlog to shape Phase 2. The intended implementation boundary is a new sync wizard started from Personal Information, with identifier selection and provider selection handled as separate steps. Provider discovery and readiness validation should live in a dedicated interoperability module rather than inside the profile screen. The backlog item for Phase 2 was updated to reflect this narrowed scope.

Tasks:
